req.title: Interest accrual (negative variant)
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-066 requires daily tier-rate interest accrual and monthly crediting on the last business day via POST /cron/interest. The violation was test[3]: kind=negative but heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is not a negative heuristic; the idempotency test is better modelled as kind=positive (it verifies correct system behaviour across state transitions) with heuristic=STC. Fix: change test[3] kind from 'negative' to 'positive'. All other tests are preserved unchanged. The suite covers: (1) EP positive – eligible account receives correct interest credit and ledger entry; (2) NEG negative – zero-balance account triggers reconciliation-variance; (3) BVA boundary – balance exactly at tier boundary receives interest at the crossing rate; (4) STC positive – cron job is idempotent so a second invocation on the same day does not double-credit; (5) DT negative – unauthorised/locked account is skipped and variance is logged.

tests.name: test_interest_credited_eligible_account
preconditions: A savings account with balance > 0 and status=ACTIVE exists
Today is the last business day of the month
The cron job has NOT run for this account today
System auth token has CRON_EXEC scope
actions: Seed account via <fixture: seed_active_savings_account> with balance=10000.00, tier='STANDARD'
Fetch ledger entries for the account via <helper_fn: get_ledger_entries>
expected: HTTP 200 with body containing {"status": "ok", "accounts_processed": >= 1}
Ledger contains exactly one new INTEREST_CREDIT entry for the account
Credit amount equals balance * daily_rate * days_in_month (within 0.01 tolerance)
Account balance is increased by the credited amount
trace: R-FIN-066: interest credited monthly on last business day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import math
from decimal import Decimal

def test_interest_credited_eligible_account(api_client, seed_active_savings_account, get_ledger_entries, cron_auth_headers, last_business_day_freeze):
    account = seed_active_savings_account(balance="10000.00", tier="STANDARD", status="ACTIVE")
    account_id = account["account_id"]
    daily_rate = Decimal(account["daily_rate"])
    days = int(account["accrual_days"])
    expected_interest = (Decimal("10000.00") * daily_rate * days).quantize(Decimal("0.01"))

    resp = api_client.post("/cron/interest", headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "ok"
    assert body["accounts_processed"] >= 1

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 1, f"Expected 1 credit entry, got {len(entries)}"
    credited = Decimal(str(entries[0]["amount"]))
    assert abs(credited - expected_interest) <= Decimal("0.01")

    updated = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    new_bal = Decimal(str(updated["balance"]))
    assert new_bal == Decimal("10000.00") + credited

tests.name: test_zero_balance_account_triggers_reconciliation_variance
preconditions: A savings account with balance=0.00 and status=ACTIVE exists
Today is the last business day of the month
System auth token has CRON_EXEC scope
actions: Seed account via <fixture: seed_active_savings_account> with balance=0.00, tier='STANDARD'
expected: HTTP 200 – job completes without server error
No INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry is created for the zero-balance account
Reconciliation report contains a VARIANCE entry referencing the account
Account balance remains 0.00
tests.executable:
import pytest
from decimal import Decimal

def test_zero_balance_account_triggers_reconciliation_variance(api_client, seed_active_savings_account, get_ledger_entries, get_reconciliation_report, cron_auth_headers, last_business_day_freeze):
    account = seed_active_savings_account(balance="0.00", tier="STANDARD", status="ACTIVE")
    account_id = account["account_id"]

    resp = api_client.post("/cron/interest", headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 0, "No interest should be credited on zero-balance account"

    report = get_reconciliation_report(run_id=resp.json()["run_id"])
    variance_ids = [v["account_id"] for v in report["variances"]]
    assert account_id in variance_ids, "Expected variance entry for zero-balance account"

    updated = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    assert Decimal(str(updated["balance"])) == Decimal("0.00")

tests.name: test_interest_rate_at_tier_boundary_balance
preconditions: Tier boundary is defined at exactly 10000.00 (STANDARD -> PREMIUM)
Account balance is seeded at exactly 10000.00
Today is the last business day of the month
System auth token has CRON_EXEC scope
actions: Seed account via <fixture: seed_active_savings_account> with balance=10000.00 (exact boundary)
Retrieve PREMIUM tier daily rate via <helper_fn: get_tier_rate>
Fetch ledger entries for the account
expected: HTTP 200
One INTEREST_CREDIT entry exists for the account
Credit amount is calculated using PREMIUM tier rate (not STANDARD)
Balance is updated to 10000.00 + interest at PREMIUM rate
trace: R-FIN-066: interest accrued at tier rate
R-FIN-066: boundary between STANDARD and PREMIUM tier at 10000.00
tests.executable:
import pytest
from decimal import Decimal

def test_interest_rate_at_tier_boundary_balance(api_client, seed_active_savings_account, get_ledger_entries, get_tier_rate, cron_auth_headers, last_business_day_freeze):
    boundary_balance = Decimal("10000.00")
    account = seed_active_savings_account(balance=str(boundary_balance), tier="AUTO", status="ACTIVE")
    account_id = account["account_id"]
    days = int(account["accrual_days"])
    premium_rate = get_tier_rate(tier="PREMIUM")
    expected_interest = (boundary_balance * premium_rate * days).quantize(Decimal("0.01"))

    resp = api_client.post("/cron/interest", headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 1
    credited = Decimal(str(entries[0]["amount"]))
    assert abs(credited - expected_interest) <= Decimal("0.01"), (
        f"Expected PREMIUM rate credit {expected_interest}, got {credited}"
    )

    updated = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    assert Decimal(str(updated["balance"])) == boundary_balance + credited

tests.name: test_cron_job_idempotent_no_double_credit
preconditions: A savings account with balance > 0 and status=ACTIVE exists
Today is the last business day of the month
The cron job has already run successfully once today for this account
System auth token has CRON_EXEC scope
actions: Seed account via <fixture: seed_active_savings_account> with balance=5000.00
Fetch ledger entries for the account
expected: Both HTTP responses return 200
Second response body contains {"status": "skipped"} or {"already_processed": true}
Exactly ONE INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry exists (no double-credit)
Account balance reflects only a single interest credit
trace: R-FIN-066: idempotency – second cron invocation on same day must not double-credit
tests.executable:
import pytest
from decimal import Decimal

def test_cron_job_idempotent_no_double_credit(api_client, seed_active_savings_account, get_ledger_entries, cron_auth_headers, last_business_day_freeze):
    account = seed_active_savings_account(balance="5000.00", tier="STANDARD", status="ACTIVE")
    account_id = account["account_id"]

    resp1 = api_client.post("/cron/interest", headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    assert resp1.json()["status"] == "ok"

    resp2 = api_client.post("/cron/interest", headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body2.get("status") == "skipped" or body2.get("already_processed") is True, (
        f"Second invocation should be skipped, got: {body2}"
    )

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 1, f"Expected exactly 1 credit entry after two invocations, got {len(entries)}"

    updated = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    credited = Decimal(str(entries[0]["amount"]))
    assert Decimal(str(updated["balance"])) == Decimal("5000.00") + credited

tests.name: test_locked_account_skipped_and_variance_logged
preconditions: A savings account with status=LOCKED exists with balance > 0
Today is the last business day of the month
System auth token has CRON_EXEC scope
actions: Seed account via <fixture: seed_active_savings_account> with balance=8000.00, status='LOCKED'
Fetch ledger entries for the locked account
expected: HTTP 200 – job completes without server error
No INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry created for the locked account
Reconciliation report contains a VARIANCE entry referencing the locked account
Account balance remains unchanged at 8000.00
trace: R-FIN-066: negative path – unauthorised/locked account skipped
tests.executable:
import pytest
from decimal import Decimal

def test_locked_account_skipped_and_variance_logged(api_client, seed_active_savings_account, get_ledger_entries, get_reconciliation_report, cron_auth_headers, last_business_day_freeze):
    account = seed_active_savings_account(balance="8000.00", tier="STANDARD", status="LOCKED")
    account_id = account["account_id"]

    resp = api_client.post("/cron/interest", headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 0, "Locked account must not receive an interest credit"

    report = get_reconciliation_report(run_id=resp.json()["run_id"])
    variance_ids = [v["account_id"] for v in report["variances"]]
    assert account_id in variance_ids, "Expected variance entry for locked account"

    updated = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    assert Decimal(str(updated["balance"])) == Decimal("8000.00")
